message:
So there I was during last month's storm—my whole block dark, neighbors wandering around with their phones at 2% battery—and I actually *had my act together* for once. Like, genuinely. I dug out the lantern I'd grabbed on sale months ago, plugged in a power bank I'd forgotten I owned, and suddenly I was the house with actual light and a way to keep everyone's phones alive. My sister texted asking if she could come charge her device, and I felt like I'd won some kind of preparedness lottery.

It's funny how a blackout flips the script. Usually I'm the person frantically borrowing stuff from prepared friends. But this time? I just... had it. And honestly, it wasn't some big dramatic thing—just a couple of smart buys sitting in a closet, waiting to be useful.

Made me realize: you don't need to overhaul your whole life to feel ready. Sometimes it's just one or two things that make the difference when the lights go out.

What's the one prep thing you've got that's actually saved you in a pinch?
